message:
refactor(guard): simplify reservation query and fix edge cases in pre-commit hook

- Move released_ts and expires_ts filtering from SQL to Python-side
  `is_released()` and `is_expired()` helpers, eliminating fragile SQL
  string-type gymnastics and making the logic testable/debuggable.
- Include `released_ts` in the SELECT so Python can inspect it.
- Handle root directory reservations (empty normalized pattern) as
  conflicting with everything instead of silently skipping.
- Change Rust `is_expired` fallback from fail-open (treat unparseable
  as expired) to fail-closed (treat as NOT expired), preventing
  accidental bypass of active reservations with unusual timestamp formats.
- Remove redundant unit tests for functions already covered by
  integration tests (resolve_hooks_dir, install/uninstall_guard,
  is_expired boundary, legacy guard detection, expand_user, render
  scripts, contains_glob).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
